name: Albert Chen
title: Software Engineering Junior / Agent Developer
motto: Aspiring to contribute to the field of AI Agents.

about.title: About
about.p1: As a third-year Software Engineering student, I am deeply passionate about the potential of AI Agents and their application in solving real-world problems. My academic journey and personal projects are geared towards building a strong foundation in this innovative field.
about.p2: My main focus these days is on developing practical skills in agent development, machine learning, and full-stack engineering. I am always seeking new challenges and opportunities to learn, grow, and contribute to impactful projects.

items.title: Digital Product & Intelligent Process Intern
description: Designed and developed RPA+AI automation flows for B2B applications, including 'Intelligent News Filtering' and 'Bulk Corporate Information Retrieval'.
Utilized RPA to simulate manual operations and integrated AI modules (Python scripts) to automate web scraping, classification, and structured data export from platforms like Dow Jones.
Researched low-code AI Agent platforms (e.g., Coze) and conducted functional testing for an 'Intelligent Data Query' product, ensuring delivery quality by writing and executing test cases.

items.name: LangChain SQL Generator Agent
items.description: This project automatically converts natural language questions into SQL queries and executes them, helping non-technical users interact directly with databases. It combines advanced NLP with a flexible agent architecture to enhance scalability and user experience.

items.name: National Mathematical Modeling Competition (H Prize)
items.description: As team leader, developed an agricultural decision optimization model for crop planting. Engineered a data pipeline with Python (pandas) and Excel, and built a Mixed Integer Programming (MIP) model using PuLP to handle over 10 constraints, optimizing solutions with a simulated annealing algorithm.

items.topic: Deep Learning-Assisted EEG Diagnosis System for Alzheimer's
description: Utilized a hybrid CNN-Transformer-MLP model to analyze EEG data, aiming to improve Alzheimer's disease prediction accuracy.
Led the full data processing pipeline from raw data to analysis-ready using EEGLab (Matlab) and MNE-Python.
Independently developed a front-end interface with HTML/CSS/JS for real-time data rendering and user interaction, delivering the project within one week.
Based on the results, a second project was established to generalize the model for other conditions like Parkinson's disease.

items.courses: Relevant Courses: Object-Oriented Programming (Python), Computer Networks, Large-Scale Database Systems, Software System Design.
